claim: Microsoft PromptWizard automatically optimizes prompts through iterative feedback loops where models critique their own outputs and refine instructions and examples
actionable: Use PromptWizard framework to automatically iterate on prompts by having the LLM generate critiques and synthesize better instructions based on test performance
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Prompt optimization should jointly optimize both instructions and in-context learning examples rather than tuning them separately
actionable: When optimizing prompts, tune instructions and few-shot examples together through multiple feedback cycles rather than treating them as independent variables
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Synthetic chain-of-thought examples generated during prompt optimization improve model reasoning more than human-written examples alone
actionable: Use prompt optimization to generate synthetic reasoning examples that capture domain-specific problem-solving patterns, then validate them before including in prompts
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Meta-prompts that suggest thinking styles ('How could I devise an experiment') can be mutated to generate diverse prompt variations for evaluation
actionable: Create meta-prompts with thinking styles that encourage diverse mutation directions, then score mutations to identify which thinking patterns work best
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Starting simple (just 'let's think step by step') and iteratively refining through critiques produces more robust prompts than starting with complex instructions
actionable: Begin with minimal prompts and let optimization frameworks add specificity through rounds of critique and synthesis rather than starting with comprehensive instructions
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Using datasets like GSM8K with PromptWizard generates task-specific prompts that encode domain knowledge (percentages, proportions) automatically without manual engineering
actionable: Feed domain-specific benchmark datasets to PromptWizard to automatically discover and encode task-relevant instructions and examples
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: LLMs struggle to generate good evals without extensive rubrics and human review, so synthetic eval generation needs external guidance
actionable: When using LLMs to generate evals, provide detailed rubrics and conduct human review of generated examples before relying on them for evaluation
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!

claim: Prompt optimization frameworks enable different model versions to use optimized prompts without hand-tuning, saving time when new models release monthly
actionable: When new models release, run your benchmark through PromptWizard automatically to get optimized prompts instead of manually tuning for each new model
source_title: How to OPTIMIZE your prompts for better Reasoning!
